comments.text: From the way the problem is written about in the cited sources, I believe the question should say 'Let $f(n)$ be maximal such that there exists a set of $n$ points in $\mathbb{R}^2$ which contains $f(n)$ many sets of four points which are 'degenerate' in the sense that some pair are the same distance apart'. Personally I don't find the phrase 'in the sense that some pair are the same distance apart' very clear and prefer something like 'in the sense that the set of 4 points induce less than 6 distinct distances'.
